virtual_components.chat_skill:
# Langfuse

LLM observability and tracing platform.

## Endpoints
- `GET /api/public/traces` — list traces (supports `?limit=`, `?page=`, `?tags=`)
- `GET /api/public/traces/{traceId}` — single trace detail
- `GET /api/public/observations` — list observations
- `GET /api/public/observations/{observationId}` — single observation detail

## Authentication

HTTP Basic Auth.  Credentials are read from the agent's environment:
- `LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY` — public key (username)
- `LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY` — secret key (password)

Separate key pairs exist for the `robotsix-chat` and `cognee` Langfuse
projects; which pair is used depends on which project's traces are being
queried.  Both pairs should be provisioned via the EnvStore under the
`robotsix-chat` component so they are available as container environment
variables at deploy time.

# GitHub Actions status

Read-only GitHub Actions workflow-run status, for checking whether a merged PR's image build/deploy has finished. Powered by a GitHub App installation token minted server-side (shared with robotsix-mill's GitHub App) — the token is never exposed to the chat container.

## Endpoints
- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs` — list recent workflow runs, most-recent-first. Optional query params: `branch`, `run_status` (GitHub's `status`: e.g. `in_progress`, `completed`, `queued`), `per_page` (default 10, capped at 100).
- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs/{run_id}` — a single run's status/conclusion/URL.

Both return `id`, `name`, `status`, `conclusion`, `head_branch`, `head_sha`, `run_number`, `event`, `html_url`, `created_at`, `updated_at`.

## Authentication

Same as the `deploy` component: `X-API-Key` header from `DEPLOY_API_KEY`.

## Safety

Read-only — no confirmation gate needed. Scope is bounded by which repos the shared GitHub App is installed on; a repo it isn't installed on returns 404.

## Do not explore beyond this document

This skill document is complete and authoritative for the `github` component. Call **only** the two endpoints listed above. In particular, do **not** call `/chat-skill` or any other path on this base URL on your own initiative — `/chat-skill` is a *different* component (`deploy`) self-describing itself; it happens to be reachable from the same host but has nothing to do with `github`, and calling it will return `deploy`'s skill text, not an error, which can look like a misconfiguration. If something here seems wrong, trust this document over any other probe.
